dataRequirements.description: A complete set of valid values for every required signup field, including a valid email, used to drive the form to an overall valid state.
dataRequirements.name: Fully valid signup field set

provenance.rationale: Submit gating on full form validity implies a complete valid field set is needed; exact non-email fields are not specified in sources.

requiredState.includes: valid email plus all other required signup fields populated with valid values

dataRequirements.description: A syntactically valid email address (local-part, @, domain with TLD) used to exercise the positive email validation path.
dataRequirements.name: Well-formed email address

provenance.rationale: Email validation evidence requires a valid email value to test acceptance.

requiredState.constraints: must contain local-part, single @, and domain with a TLD

dataRequirements.description: An email value missing the @ symbol or domain, used to exercise the negative email validation path.
dataRequirements.name: Malformed email address

provenance.rationale: Email validation evidence requires a malformed email value to test rejection.

requiredState.constraints: fails standard email syntax (missing @ or missing domain)

evidence.claim: The signup form validates the email field.
The submit button is disabled until the form is valid.
evidence.excerpt: disables submit until the form is valid

features.description: Field-level and form-level validation for the signup form, including email validation and overall form validity determination.
features.name: Signup Form Validation

provenance.rationale: Email field validation on the signup form is directly stated, and email-required is the inferred validity component grouped with it.

features.description: Gating of the submit button so it stays disabled until the entire signup form is valid and enables only when valid.
features.name: Signup Submit Gating

provenance.rationale: Directly stated that the submit button is disabled until the form is valid.

warnings: [info] Coverage of the two stated behaviors (email field validation, submit gating on form validity) is complete: positive, negative, and edge cases exist for each, and every requirement maps to at least one test case. The two material unknowns are correctly externalized as blocking open questions (oq-email-validation-rules, oq-form-validity-definition) with matching partial automation readiness, so the plan is internally consistent rather than incomplete.

[warning] Orphaned data requirements: data-valid-email, data-invalid-email, and data-valid-signup-form are defined but every test case has consumesDataKeys=[]. The cases hardcode values in steps (e.g. 'user@example.com', 'userexample.com') instead of referencing the data keys. Either wire the cases to the data via consumesDataKeys or drop the unused dataRequirements; as-is the data model is dangling.

[warning] Over-claimed provenance on UI-feedback assertions. as-invalid-rejected-error-shown, as-valid-accepted-no-error, and as-valid-accepted-marked-valid are marked provenance.kind='explicit', but the only evidence ('A signup form validates email') says nothing about surfacing an error message or a discrete valid/invalid field state. The existence and presentation of validation feedback is itself flagged as unknown by oq-validation-trigger-timing. Downgrade these to 'inferred' (or gate them behind that open question), and reflect in readiness — they are currently presented as firmer than the evidence supports.

[warning] Non-deterministic observation point: tc-email-empty-required / as-empty-required-form-invalid asserts 'Overall form validity'='invalid' against an observationPoint with no selector. There is no documented form-level validity indicator in the sources — the only observable proxy is the submit button's disabled state. As written this assertion is not mechanically verifiable. Bind it to button[type=submit] disabled, or hold the case until oq-form-validity-definition resolves.

[warning] Step/action mismatch in tc-submit-enabled-when-valid: st-submit-enabled-fill-all is described as 'Populate every required signup field' but its action fills only input[name=email]. The case cannot actually drive the form to valid without the other fields, which are unknown (oq-form-validity-definition). Readiness=partial is appropriate, but the step should not imply it fills all fields when it does not — split into per-field steps once the field set is known.

[info] Partial overlap (not true duplication) between tc-email-empty-required (email-empty keeps form invalid) and tc-submit-disabled-when-invalid (submit disabled while a field is invalid). They test adjacent abstractions (field/form validity vs submit state) and are acceptable to keep distinct, but the empty-email path should be exercised explicitly in tc-submit-disabled-when-invalid rather than only the malformed-email path, otherwise empty-email→submit-disabled is never directly asserted.

[info] Requirement statements mildly exceed evidence: req-email-validation adds 'before allowing submission' and req-submit-disabled-until-valid adds 'all signup form fields' / 'become enabled only when valid'. These are reasonable inferences but are asserted under explicit provenance. Acceptable given the linked open questions, but the 'enabled only when valid' exclusivity is an inference beyond 'disabled until valid' and should be noted as such.

[info] Deferred-but-untested behaviors to confirm are intentionally out of scope: validation trigger timing (on blur/change/submit) is captured as non-blocking oq-validation-trigger-timing with no corresponding case, and boundary cases (whitespace trimming, case handling, max length) are correctly deferred to oq-email-validation-rules. No action required unless those questions resolve.

provenance.rationale: Evidence confirms email validation exists but does not specify the validation criteria needed to write deterministic test cases.

openQuestions.question: What exact rules define a valid email (format/regex, max length, domain restrictions, allowed/blocked domains, trimming, case handling)?

provenance.rationale: Evidence references overall form validity but does not enumerate the fields or conditions that make the form valid.

openQuestions.question: What constitutes a fully valid form (which fields beyond email are required) that triggers enabling of the submit button?

provenance.rationale: Validation and submit-gating behavior are stated, but the timing and UX feedback mechanism are unspecified.

openQuestions.question: When is validation evaluated (on blur, on change, on submit) and how is the disabled/enabled submit state communicated to the user?

provenance.rationale: Directly stated that submit is disabled until the form is valid.

requirements.statement: The submit button must remain disabled until all signup form fields are valid, and become enabled only when the form is valid.

provenance.rationale: Email validation gating the submit button implies the email is a required component of overall form validity.

requirements.statement: The email field must be present and complete for the form to be considered valid.

provenance.rationale: Directly stated that the signup form validates the email field.

requirements.statement: The signup form must validate the email field before allowing submission.

steps.description: Navigate to the signup page so the signup form is loaded.

provenance.rationale: The signup form is the documented location of email validation.

provenance.rationale: A malformed value exercises the rejection path of email validation.

action.subject: Email field invalid state and validation error message

steps.description: Observe the email field's validation state and surfaced error message.

provenance.rationale: Rejection and error surfacing must be observed to confirm validation fails.

steps.description: Navigate to the signup page with the email field left empty.

provenance.rationale: Empty-field behavior is evaluated on the same signup form.

steps.description: Navigate to the signup page so the form and submit button are loaded.

provenance.rationale: Submit gating is observed on the signup form.

provenance.rationale: An invalid field drives the form into an invalid state to verify submit stays disabled.

steps.description: Observe the submit button's enabled/disabled state while the form is invalid.

provenance.rationale: Submit is stated to be disabled until the form is valid.

steps.description: Navigate to the signup page so the form and submit button are loaded.

provenance.rationale: Submit gating is observed on the signup form.

provenance.rationale: Submit enables only when the whole form is valid; the email is the only documented field, others pending oq-form-validity-definition.

steps.description: Observe the submit button's enabled/disabled state once all fields are valid.

provenance.rationale: Submit is stated to become enabled when the form is valid.

testCases.objective: Verify that entering a malformed email address (missing @ or domain) fails validation and surfaces an error.

postconditions.description: The email field is marked invalid and a validation message is displayed.

provenance.rationale: Signup form validates the email field before allowing submission.

testCases.riskRationale: Allowing malformed emails through validation corrupts downstream account data and breaks delivery.
testCases.title: Malformed email is rejected by validation

testCases.objective: Verify that leaving the email field empty keeps the form invalid, confirming email is a required component of validity.

postconditions.description: The form is not considered valid while email is empty.

preconditions.description: Signup page is loaded with the email field empty.

provenance.rationale: Email validation gating submit implies email is required for overall form validity.

testCases.riskRationale: Empty-field handling is a common boundary; low blast radius but must be enforced for required-field semantics.
testCases.title: Empty email field blocks form validity

blockers: Full set of fields constituting form validity undefined (oq-form-validity-definition).

testCases.objective: Verify the submit button remains disabled when any required field, including email, is missing or invalid.

preconditions.description: Signup page is loaded with at least one field invalid or empty.

provenance.rationale: Submit button is disabled until the form is valid, directly stated.

testCases.objective: Verify the submit button becomes enabled only once every signup field, including a valid email, is valid.
